920: [Color:0xc]◆Summary[Color:0x0]
  An artificial gene angels inserted into 
  those who chose to live above the Ceiling.
[Color:0xc]◆Signs of Infection[Color:0x0] 
  The condition progresses in three stages. 
    1. Asymptomatic stage (incubation period)
      It is believed that 90% of Mikado's
      population carries the Demon Gene, 
      but it is asymptomatic unless certain 
      conditions are met.
    2. Onset
      *”Archangels are not nearby” 
      *”Infected have progressed to a certain
      point of cultural development” 
      Meeting the above conditions causes 
      the onset of the Demon Gene. Infection
      can also spread communally, without
      meeting the conditions stated above.
      Infection causes the reddening of
      eyes and the cerebral overactivity. 
      Excess dopamine causes hyperactivity
      and the feeding center of the brain, 
      compelling cannibalism.
    3. Complete infection 
      Further progression spreads from excessive
      cerebral activity to the rest of the body,
      causing transformation. This may cause
      increased strength and the development of
      abilities at great risk to the host's body.       
[Color:0xc]Prevention[Color:0x0]
  The Eastern Kingdom of Mikado's settlers
  were genetically modified by the angels.
  The Demon Gene since passed down from
  generation to generation. When people
  from Tokyo broke through the Ceiling and
  joined the Mikado population, the Demon 
  Gene was bred out of a small percentage.
  Theories suggest the contagion spreads
  through biological magnetite, but concrete
  details are too lacking to be sure.
  
[END]
930: [Color:0xc]◆Summary[Color:0x0]
  Hugo as transformed by the Demon
  Gene. Formerly the head of the
  Eastern Kingdom of Mikado's Monastery.
  (Please view entry on [Color:0x5]Hugo[Color:0x0] for more 
  details.)
  

  He is the first person to become 
  transformed by the Demon Gene. 
  After full onset, he becomes one with 
  YHVH's Universe and assumes the role 
  of God's messenger. He strived to 
  spread the Demon Gene. Those he 
  infected fell under his control. 
  Hugo also gains an intuitive insight into
  the location of those he infects. The 
  infected cannot be freed while the
  one controlling them still lives. [Flynn]
  eventually defeated Hugo, ending the
  threat of a Demon Gene epidemic.
[END]
